name: usd_deposit_3m
description: QuantLib-parity USD 3M money-market deposit on a committed discount curve.

provenance.source_detail: QuantLib-Python 1.42.1 parity check using IborIndex forecast fixing for deposit par rate, CashFlows.npv for start and maturity flows, and central finite-difference DV01 on a deterministic USD discount curve. Phase-2 envelope migration (2026-05-09): wrapped in market_envelope with the curve carried in initial_market.curves and an empty plan.steps. The QuantLib oracle's synthetic time pillars are not bootstrap-reproducible; true-calibration migration would require >1e-12 par_rate tolerance. The envelope wrap still routes through engine::execute_with_diagnostics, so the fixture exercises the calibration entry point's plumbing.
provenance.regen_command: manual QuantLib-Python oracle; see inputs.source_reference for reproduction formulas and intermediate values

plan.description: Tier-A wrap: deposit is priced against a pre-built USD-OIS curve carried in initial_market. The curve cannot be bootstrapped from a real-date deposit quote at par_rate tolerance 1e-12 because the snapshot uses synthetic time pillars (0.25, 0.5, ..., 5.0) that don't align with the calibration engine's date arithmetic. Routing through engine::execute_with_diagnostics still validates the envelope schema and the calibration entry point with no calibration-side math change.

source_reference.note: QuantLib oracle: par rate follows IborIndex::forecastFixing, (DF(start) / DF(end) - 1) / Act360 year fraction; NPV follows CashFlows.npv over the -notional start flow and notional * (1 + quote_rate * year_fraction) maturity flow. Because this fixture uses Finstack synthetic time knots, the QuantLib reproduction is fed the maturity discount factor implied by the fixture's Act/365F log-linear discount curve interpolation.
